title: Trouble Diagnosis — Vehicle Creeps

figures_detected.description: CONSULT tool connected in vehicle interior, showing the diagnostic connector location
figures_detected.associated_step: Note 1 — Measure clutch current procedure step 1

content_blocks.text:
Symptom: The vehicle moves slowly when shifting to driving range from a complete standstill. (When accelerator pedal is released)
NOTE: It is normal to have an almost imperceptible creep or a slight creep (less than the creep of a conventional hydraulic A/T)

Diagnosis item:
• Accelerator switch or accelerator position switch is faulty
• N-CVT control unit is faulty
• Electromagnetic clutch is faulty
• Insulation is faulty on harness between electromagnetic clutch and N-CVT control unit
(Refer to diagnosis chart on following page)

nodes.text:
Check accelerator switch circuit
Chart reference number: 1
Repair or replace accelerator switch, accelerator position switch or harness

Measure clutch current
Chart reference number: 2,3
[Note 1]
Check or replace brush holder harness.

steps.text: Pull handbrake securely, depress brake pedal, shift selector lever from "N" to "D" range while idling. Read "ACTUAL CURRENT" value on screen. Standard value: 0.1 - 0.2 A
Measure current while depressing and releasing the accelerator pedal after having fulfilled conditions mentioned under item 3. (Set accelerator switch ON and OFF)

items.label: Clutch current — Accelerator pedal is released (Accelerator switch "ON")
items.condition: Selector in D, engine idling, handbrake applied, accelerator pedal released

items.label: Clutch current — Accelerator pedal is depressed (Accelerator switch "OFF")
items.condition: Selector in D, engine idling, handbrake applied, accelerator pedal depressed

tools_referenced.name: CONSULT diagnostic tool
tools_referenced.usage: Connect to vehicle to monitor "ACTUAL CURRENT" via DATA MONITOR function

model_notes: N-CVT (automatic transmission variant). Creep behaviour note: slight creep less than conventional hydraulic A/T is normal.
